body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Current keywords in template.package.json ==="
rg '"keywords"' -A 20 .config/template.package.json | head -25

echo -e "\n=== Markdown mentions in README ==="
rg -i 'markdown' README.md | head -10

echo -e "\n=== General syntax highlighting/language support mentions ==="
rg -i '(syntax.?highlight|shiki|language.?support)' README.md | head -10

echo -e "\n=== Platform/roadmap mentions ==="
rg -i '(windows|linux|macos|platform|roadmap)' README.md | head -15
```

Repository: appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode

Length of output: 2307

---



</details>

**Reconsider keyword additions—all three raise valid points.**

- "document printing" is redundant. You already have "print", "code printing", and "print preview" covering that space.
- "markdown" doesn't belong as a dedicated keyword. The README emphasizes Shiki and syntax highlighting broadly—markdown gets one mention as one of 100+ supported languages. It doesn't warrant standalone prominence.
- "macos" won't age well. Your roadmap explicitly lists "Windows & Linux (Planned)" with OSWin/OSLinux abstractions already in code. Remove the keyword or plan to yank it later.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In .config/template.package.json around lines 24 to 26, the added keywords
"document printing", "markdown", and "macos" are unnecessary or potentially
problematic; remove "document printing" (redundant with existing "print", "code
printing", "print preview"), remove "markdown" (not a primary keyword given
Shiki/syntax-highlighting focus), and remove "macos" (OS-specific keyword that
may become stale); update the keywords array to omit these three entries and
ensure remaining keywords accurately reflect long-term scope.
```

</details>

+    branches: [ main ]
+  pull_request:

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Fix YAML formatting issues.**

Your workflow has YAML linting errors: truthy value formatting on line 3 and excess bracket spacing on lines 5 and 7. Fix them or yamllint will complain.



<details>
<summary>🔎 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-on:
+true:
   push:
-    branches: [ main ]
+    branches: [main]
   pull_request:
-    branches: [ main ]
+    branches: [main]
```

Wait, that's wrong. The actual fix:

```diff
-on:
+on: # yamllint disable-line rule:truthy
   push:
-    branches: [ main ]
+    branches: [main]
   pull_request:
-    branches: [ main ]
+    branches: [main]
```

Or just use the list syntax:

```diff
 on:
   push:
-    branches: [ main ]
+    branches:
+      - main
   pull_request:
-    branches: [ main ]
+    branches:
+      - main
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
on: # yamllint disable-line rule:truthy
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🧰 Tools</summary>

<details>

[warning] 3-3: truthy value should be one of [false, true]

(truthy)

---

[error] 5-5: too many spaces inside brackets

(brackets)

---

[error] 5-5: too many spaces inside brackets

(brackets)

---

[error] 7-7: too many spaces inside brackets

(brackets)

---

[error] 7-7: too many spaces inside brackets

(brackets)

</details>

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
In .github/workflows/ci.yml around lines 3-7, the workflow has YAML formatting
issues: remove the space inside the inline list brackets or, preferably, replace
inline bracket lists with proper YAML list entries; change "branches: [ main ]"
to either "branches: [main]" or the multiline form "branches:" followed by "-
```

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Upgrade codecov action to v4.**

You're using `codecov/codecov-action@v3` which runs on a deprecated Node.js 16 runner. GitHub Actions will eventually stop supporting this. Upgrade to v4.



<details>
<summary>🔎 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
       - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
-        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
+        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
         if: matrix.node-version == '20.x'
         with:
           files: ./coverage/coverage-final.json
           fail_ci_if_error: false
```

Note: v4 may require a `CODECOV_TOKEN` secret. Check the docs.
</details>

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Add secret validation and error handling.**

Line 40 uses `secrets.VSCE_PAT` without checking if it exists. If the secret is missing or invalid, the workflow fails silently or leaks error details.



<details>
<summary>🔎 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
   - name: Publish to VS Code Marketplace
+    if: env.VSCE_PAT != ''
     run: vsce publish ${{ github.event.inputs.version || 'patch' }}
     env:
       VSCE_PAT: ${{ secrets.VSCE_PAT }}
+  
+  - name: Verify secret configured
+    if: env.VSCE_PAT == ''
+    run: |
+      echo "::error::VSCE_PAT secret is not configured"
+      exit 1
```
</details>

<details>

```
.github/workflows/publish.yml lines 37-40: the workflow uses secrets.VSCE_PAT
directly when running vsce publish which can lead to silent failures or leaking
details if the secret is missing/invalid; update the job to validate the secret
before calling vsce (fail fast with a clear masked error if it's empty), avoid
printing the secret anywhere, and exit with non-zero status on missing/invalid
secret so the workflow fails explicitly; optionally mark the secret as required
```

+# Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
+
+## Our Pledge
+
+We as members, contributors, and leaders pledge to make participation in our
+community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body
+size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender
+identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status,
+nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, or sexual
+identity and orientation.
+
+We pledge to act and interact in ways that contribute to an open, welcoming,
+diverse, inclusive, and healthy community.
+
+## Our Standards
+
+Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment for our
+community include:
+
+* Demonstrating empathy and kindness toward other people
+* Being respectful of differing opinions, viewpoints, and experiences
+* Giving and gracefully accepting constructive feedback
+* Accepting responsibility and apologizing to those affected by our mistakes,
+  and learning from the experience
+* Focusing on what is best not just for us as individuals, but for the
+  overall community
+
+Examples of unacceptable behavior include:
+
+* The use of sexualized language or imagery, and sexual attention or
+  advances of any kind
+* Trolling, insulting or derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks
+* Public or private harassment
+* Publishing others' private information, such as a physical or email
+  address, without their explicit permission
+* Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a
+  professional setting
+
+## Enforcement Responsibilities
+
+Community leaders are responsible for clarifying and enforcing our standards of
+acceptable behavior and will take appropriate and fair corrective action in
+response to any behavior that they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive,
+or harmful.
+
+Community leaders have the right and responsibility to remove, edit, or reject
+comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that are
+not aligned to this Code of Conduct, and will communicate reasons for moderation
+decisions when appropriate.
+
+## Scope
+
+This Code of Conduct applies within all community spaces, and also applies when
+an individual is officially representing the community in public spaces.
+Examples of representing our community include using an official e-mail address,
+posting via an official social media account, or acting as an appointed
+representative at an online or offline event.
+
+## Enforcement
+
+Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be
+reported to the community leaders responsible for enforcement at
+[INSERT CONTACT METHOD].

**Fill in the contact method placeholder.**

Line 63 has "[INSERT CONTACT METHOD]" placeholder. You can't publish community governance with a placeholder—it renders the enforcement section useless.

+# Contributing to Print2Paper4VSCode
+
+First off, thanks for taking the time to contribute!
+
+## How to Contribute
+
+1. **Fork the repository** and clone it locally.
+2. **Create a branch** for your edits.
+3. **Make your changes** and adhere to the coding standards (see `docs/AGENTS.md`).
+4. **Run tests** to ensure no regressions: `npm test`.
+5. **Submit a Pull Request** describing your changes.
+
+## Development Setup
+
+See [INSTALL.md](docs/INSTALL.md) for detailed setup instructions.
+
+## Coding Standards
+
+- Use TypeScript with strict mode.
+- Follow the Registry pattern for dependency injection.
+- Ensure all tests pass.
+- Run `npm run lint` and `npm run format` before committing.
+
+## Pull Request Process
+
+1. Update the README.md with details of changes to the interface, this includes new environment variables, exposed ports, useful file locations and container parameters.
